quoteFirst: He used to be night watchman at Oak Dene in old Mr Morice's time. Sometimes he takes the letter-bags to and from the mail train, which goes through at
quoteLast: in the morning. He did so last night. He assures me with his own lips that, coming home, as he was passing your place, he heard something moving, and on looking round saw you among the trees in your nightdress.
title: Miss Arnott's Marriage
author: Marsh, Richard, 1857-1915

quoteFirst: "Sure, every night exceptin' Saturday, and then it's twelve to
title: The Long Day: The Story of a New York Working Girl, as Told by Herself
author: Richardson, Dorothy, 1882-

quoteFirst: The rear squadron, commanded by Admiral Drake, now came up, and the Comte de Grasse prudently hauled his wind; and as his ships sailed better than the English, he succeeded in gradually drawing off, and by
quoteLast: his fleet were all out of the reach of shot.
title: Memoirs and Correspondence of Admiral Lord de Saumarez, Vol. I
author: Ross, John, Sir, 1777-1856

quoteFirst: The work was most dangerous, for the ground which had to be covered was continually and most heavily shelled.  From 12 a.m. to
quoteLast: the Battalion held on under the most desperate difficulties until a detachment of the 4th Rifle Brigade was sent up in reinforcement The battered defenders of the support trench recognised old friends coming to their aid in their moment of extreme trial, and gave them a loud cheer as they advanced in support.  Lieut.
title: Canada in Flanders, Volume I
author: Beaverbrook, Max Aitken, Baron, 1879-1964

quoteFirst: The Buffs had been given the two latter as objective. Very considerable machine-gun opposition was met with, but a line a hundred yards short of the objective was consolidated at
quoteLast: At 7.45 the “S.O.S.” signal went up from part of the 18th Division’s front and there was heavy artillery fire on our forward line, but no infantry action followed. Lieut. Brooke was wounded, one man killed, twenty-seven wounded and seven missing.
title: Historical records of the Buffs, East Kent Regiment (3rd Foot)
author: Moody, R. S. H. (Richard Stanley Hawks), 1854-1930; Alexander, Herbert, 1874-1946 [Artist]; Butler, Elizabeth (Elizabeth Southerden Thompson), 1846-1933 [Artist]; Paget, Arthur, 1851-1928 [Author of introduction, etc.]

quoteLast: , a distant noise like thunder made itself heard above all the human din, and then the crowd seemed to go perfectly mad, yelling and shouting like Bedlam let loose. It was time to get ready to leave anyhow, so I rose from my sleepless bed and went to the window.
title: My Spanish Year
author: Whishaw, Ellen M.

quoteFirst: Satisfied with our observations, we left the rock of Culimacari at
quoteLast: on the morning of the 12th. The torment of mosquitos, to which we were exposed, augmented in proportion as we withdrew from the Rio Negro. There are no zancudos in the valley of Cassiquiare, but the simulia, and all the other insects of the tipulary family, are the more numerous and venomous.
title: Personal Narrative of Travels to the Equinoctial Regions of America, During the Year 1799-1804 — Volume 2
author: Humboldt, Alexander von, 1769-1859; Bonpland, Aimé, 1773-1858; Ross, Thomasina [Translator]

quoteFirst: Piper had laid down her load of earthly care, Miss Scratchell restricted her visits to the Park to purely professional limits. She entered the schoolroom punctually at nine, and she left it as punctually at
quoteLast: . She no longer assisted at the children’s early dinner, a meal which Mr. Piper, when at home, shared under the name of luncheon.
title: An open verdict
author: Braddon, M. E. (Mary Elizabeth), 1835-1915

quoteFirst: Two hours were needed to re-form the line, when it was found that their numbers were now reduced to 1,300 men. With all these delays, at
quoteLast: in the morning they were still three miles from the city.

quoteFirst: The sun was shining and I was feeling pretty good. There were lots of people coming and going, and the streets outside were so crowded I thought sure there was going to be a parade. About
quoteLast: it started to rain and it rained harder than it ever rains in Texas. Of course I didn’t have any rubbers or umbrella along, and when my sister got there she didn’t have any either.
title: Won in the ninth
author: Mathewson, Christy, 1880-1925; Aulick, W. W. (Will Wroth), 1873-1913 [Editor]; Mahony, Felix, 1867-1939 [Illustrator]
